overview: Real-world teaching bundle for the Genome Forge tutorial, including public-source records and clearly labeled training derivatives.
usage_tips: Use extract_case_bundle.py to materialize the exact records for one case.
Base public-source sequences live in training_real_world_sequences.fasta.
Derived training records are generated from public-source parents using explicit edit lists stored in this JSON.

records.name: EGFP_CDS
records.origin: Engineered fluorescent reporter derived from the Aequorea victoria GFP family.
records.why_it_matters: EGFP is a canonical lab reporter: great for teaching translation, cloning, sequence verification, and how a few codons can change an optical phenotype.
records.input_details: This is a protein-coding DNA sequence (CDS). The sequence is meant to be translated from the first base, so codon boundaries and reading frame matter immediately.
records.fun_fact: A fluorescent protein is basically a self-reporting molecule: the sequence encodes the protein, and the folded protein creates its own chromophore.

records.name: mCherry_CDS
records.origin: Monomeric red fluorescent protein from the mFruit engineering lineage.
records.why_it_matters: mCherry is a real-world counterexample to GFP: same broad job as a reporter, different sequence history, color, and engineering constraints.
records.input_details: This is also a CDS, but it encodes a coral-derived red fluorescent protein rather than a GFP-family green reporter. That makes it useful for pairwise comparison and identity search.
records.fun_fact: The reason mCherry mattered so much in cell biology is that it was engineered to behave as a monomer, which made protein fusions far easier to interpret.
records.source_label: PubMed: A monomeric red fluorescent protein

records.name: pUC19_MCS
records.origin: The pUC19 multiple-cloning site from one of the most widely used teaching and cloning vectors in molecular biology.
records.why_it_matters: A dense multiple-cloning site is a perfect example of engineered sequence architecture: every base exists to make cloning more flexible.
records.input_details: This sequence is short, circularly interpreted, and packed with restriction motifs. It is intentionally synthetic and engineered for manipulation rather than natural gene expression.
records.fun_fact: The pUC19 MCS is tiny, but it changed day-to-day cloning because it made many enzyme choices available in one compact region.

records.name: lacZ_alpha_fragment
records.origin: lacZ alpha fragment from the classic blue-white screening system associated with cloning vectors such as pUC19.
records.why_it_matters: This fragment is a perfect teaching example of phenotype-linked DNA: inserting the wrong thing into the wrong place changes colony color.
records.input_details: The sequence is a cloning-era workhorse. It contains coding material, but many workflows care more about its role as a reporter module than as a standalone protein-coding fragment.
records.fun_fact: Blue-white screening is a molecular biology hack built on protein complementation: the colony color is a proxy for whether the vector was disrupted.

records.name: BRAF_exon15_fragment
records.origin: A hotspot-rich fragment centered on human BRAF exon 15, the region associated with the famous V600 oncogenic mutation family.
records.why_it_matters: This is the most medically consequential sequence in the training set. It is ideal for primer design, genotyping logic, CRISPR planning, and explaining why genomic DNA is not the same thing as a CDS.
records.input_details: This is a genomic fragment, not a clean coding-sequence input. If you translate it naively, you hit stop codons because intron/exon context and strand assumptions matter.
records.fun_fact: BRAF V600E became a textbook hotspot because one amino-acid change in a signaling kinase can rewire cell behavior so strongly that it becomes clinically actionable.

records.name: EGFP_Y67H_training_variant
records.origin: Training derivative of EGFP that changes the aromatic residue in the chromophore-forming motif.
records.why_it_matters: This is not a random mutation. It models the idea that one codon change near a chromophore can produce a large optical shift, which is a powerful lesson for variant interpretation.
records.input_details: Because this record is derived from EGFP by a single codon change, it is excellent for pairwise alignment, amino-acid consequence analysis, and demonstrating how “small diff, big phenotype” problems happen in biology.
records.fun_fact: Some of the most useful fluorescent protein families differ by only a handful of amino acids, yet those few edits can visibly change the color you see under the microscope.
records.source_label: Derived from EGFP mutant logic described in GFP engineering literature

records.name: EGFP_S204Y_training_variant
records.origin: Training derivative of EGFP that alters an aromatic-packing site near the chromophore environment.
records.why_it_matters: It gives the tutorial a second closely related variant, which makes alignment, consensus, and comparison-lens examples far more realistic than comparing unrelated proteins only.
records.input_details: Like the Y67H-like variant, this record is generated from a public-source EGFP backbone. The point is not to claim a specific commercial reagent but to give you a realistic engineering-style mutation to reason about.
records.fun_fact: Spectral tuning in fluorescent proteins often happens because the local protein environment changes how the chromophore sees electrons, not because the whole protein is redesigned.

case_inputs.title: Restriction Map for Cloning Entry Design
case_inputs.workflow: Render the pUC19 map and compare unique restriction choices before adding a reporter insert.

case_inputs.title: Methylation-Aware Digest Interpretation
case_inputs.workflow: Compare standard digest output with methylation-aware digest logic on a real cloning vector motif set.

case_inputs.title: Star Activity Risk Review
case_inputs.workflow: Scan relaxed-matching cut risk to understand how star activity can create off-target restriction events.

case_inputs.title: k-mer Profile for Contamination Suspicion
case_inputs.workflow: Use motif/entity search patterns to ask whether a supposed single-template sample smells like a mixed cloning population.

case_inputs.title: Sequence Track and Translation Context
case_inputs.workflow: Inspect EGFP with sequence tracks so base coordinates, codons, amino acids, and features can be read together.

case_inputs.title: ORF Scan and Coding Potential Triage
case_inputs.workflow: Compare a clean coding sequence and a genomic fragment to learn what ORF scanning can and cannot tell you.

case_inputs.title: Variant Annotation from Reference-Aligned Edits
case_inputs.workflow: Align a public reporter CDS to a derived chromophore variant and explain the difference in protein terms.

case_inputs.title: Protein Property Inference from Translation
case_inputs.workflow: Translate two real reporter proteins and compare what the sequence suggests about size, composition, and practical use.

case_inputs.title: Primer Design and Thermodynamic Screening
case_inputs.workflow: Design primers around the BRAF hotspot region and screen them for temperature and composition sanity.

case_inputs.title: Specificity Ranking with Virtual PCR/Gel
case_inputs.workflow: Rank candidate primer pairs against a realistic background panel and inspect the predicted gel outcome.

case_inputs.title: Multiplex PCR Panel Balancing
case_inputs.workflow: Compare multiple assay targets and ask whether they can coexist in one panel without obvious conflict.

case_inputs.title: Positive and Negative Control Design
case_inputs.workflow: Design an assay package that includes controls proving both signal presence and signal absence.

case_inputs.title: Cloning Compatibility and Ligation Product Ranking
case_inputs.workflow: Check whether the vector and insert support a coherent directional cloning plan and inspect likely ligation products.

case_inputs.title: Circular Construct Integrity and Junction Validation
case_inputs.workflow: Validate a circularized construct by focusing on junctions, scars, and reading-frame continuity.

case_inputs.title: Multi-Trace Consensus for Final Construct Call
case_inputs.workflow: Combine multiple trace-derived views into one final verdict about a reporter construct.

case_inputs.title: MSA, Identity Heatmap, and Phylogeny
case_inputs.workflow: Compare a small reporter family panel to see what is conserved, what is engineered, and what is genuinely distant.

case_inputs.title: GC Landscape and Repeat Fragility
case_inputs.workflow: Use analytics tracks to identify composition features that may complicate PCR, synthesis, or sequencing.

case_inputs.title: Homopolymer and Low-Complexity Risk Detection
case_inputs.workflow: Flag simple-sequence patches that often produce weak confidence in sequencing or synthesis workflows.

case_inputs.title: Motif Enrichment and Significance Framing
case_inputs.workflow: Compare motif density across engineered vector DNA and reporter CDS records to learn when motif count is meaningful.

case_inputs.title: CRISPR Candidate and HDR Donor Design
case_inputs.workflow: Design guide RNAs and an HDR donor around a medically meaningful BRAF hotspot region.

case_inputs.title: Promoter/RBS Context for Expression Tuning
case_inputs.workflow: Use annotation and translation context to discuss why expression output depends on more than the CDS alone.

case_inputs.title: Codon Usage Bias and Host Portability
case_inputs.workflow: Discuss how two common reporter CDS records might look to different host translation systems.

case_inputs.title: DNA Container Roundtrip Validation
case_inputs.workflow: Export and re-import multiple records to verify that file conversion preserves sequence identity and annotations.

case_inputs.title: AB1 Trace Alignment and Consensus Editing
case_inputs.workflow: Import a Sanger-style trace, align it to EGFP, perform an edit, and recompute consensus.

case_inputs.title: Read Simulation and Coverage Planning
case_inputs.workflow: Use realistic target regions to think about how much sequencing evidence is enough for a confident call.

case_inputs.title: Sequence Analytics Lens (GC, Skew, Complexity, Stop Density)
case_inputs.workflow: Use the analytics lens on a clean CDS and a genomic fragment to see how sequence context changes interpretation.

case_inputs.title: Comparison Lens (Divergence + Confidence Hotspots)
case_inputs.workflow: Visualize where two nearly identical sequences diverge and decide whether the divergence matters.

case_inputs.title: Native .dna Import and Multi-Format Conversion Workflow
case_inputs.workflow: Demonstrate that a real record can move through multiple formats and come back interpretable.

case_inputs.title: Chromatogram-First Sanger Review and Confidence Gating
case_inputs.workflow: Start with the chromatogram itself before trusting the base calls.

case_inputs.title: Trace-Based Genotyping and Plasmid Verification
case_inputs.workflow: Use trace evidence to make either a hotspot genotype call or a plasmid verification call.

case_inputs.title: BLAST-like Similarity Search for Identity, Origin, and Contamination
case_inputs.workflow: Run local similarity search against a small real-world panel to identify the most likely source of an unknown sequence.

case_inputs.title: Reference Element Auto-Flagging and siRNA Design/Mapping
case_inputs.workflow: Reuse saved element libraries to auto-flag familiar sequence elements, then design and map siRNA candidates.

case_inputs.title: Collaboration, Audit, and Review Governance
case_inputs.workflow: Create a workspace, assign roles, and run a simple review flow on a saved construct project.

case_inputs.title: Batch Reproducibility and Parameter Locking
case_inputs.workflow: Run the same logic across several records with a locked parameter set so outputs stay comparable.

case_inputs.title: Reproducible Report Package
case_inputs.workflow: Package a saved project and a share bundle so another scientist can reopen the same analysis context.

case_inputs.title: Parameter Sensitivity and Robustness Check
case_inputs.workflow: Rerun a workflow under a small parameter sweep to see whether the biological conclusion is robust or fragile.

case_inputs.title: End-to-End Release Checklist and Handoff
case_inputs.workflow: Treat the tutorial workspace like a releasable scientific software product and verify the handoff boundary.
